classification_scope: Full reconciliation through merged PR #59 and authenticated collector demo API deploy run 30044644501, including accepted isolated What-If, failed deployment-name collision, and unproven post-failure Azure target-resource state.

workspace_hierarchy.authority_boundary: Conversation context supports reasoning and routing. Live GitHub, exact-head CI, committed source and tests, protected workflow artifacts, and fresh Azure evidence determine implementation and runtime state.

durable_repository_watermark.semantics: Promoted repository watermark only; query live GitHub for the current head and open work.

promoted_Azure_watermark.authority_boundary: The artifact proves an accepted isolated plan and failed deploy attempt. It does not prove successful deployment, zero partial mutation, service operation, or authority to retry.

active_architecture.name: collector-hosted-demo-api
active_architecture.claim_boundary: The isolated root and PR #59 name repair are implemented. A fresh exact-commit inventory and What-If are required before a retry decision.

entries.claim_boundary: Durable promoted state and evidence only; current external state must be queried.
Proves workflow implementation, not dispatch, authority, or outcome.
Proves the repaired scoped resource graph, not an Azure What-If or deployment.
Proves intended public ingress and VM extension resources, not their existence or secure operation.
Recommends typed control messages without executing or granting authority.
Design and validation only; replacement, rollback, and recovery remain unproven.
Evidence representation and validation only; no operational recovery proof.

entries.allowed_when: Reviewing future workflow promotion, execution, or authority boundaries.
entries.claim_boundary: Files outside .github/workflows are not active or dispatchable.
Proves committed deterministic behavior, not live service operation.
Proves source and test contracts, not deployment or public reachability.
Live URLs remain blank until deployment and browser verification; fixture fallback is authoritative.

entries.allowed_when: Reviewing API invocation, report validation, or fallback behavior.
entries.claim_boundary: Browser behavior does not prove the configured endpoint exists.
Starting point only; verify consequential claims against live GitHub and Azure evidence.
Summaries do not replace protected raw artifacts.
Approved or reviewed intent, not deployment or operation.
Orientation only; narrower source and evidence prevail.
Historical context never overrides current source or evidence.
Must be classified before supporting a current-state claim.

resolved_repository_events.scope: separate durable history from live repository state
bound known backend NIC What-If noise
isolate collector API from base infrastructure
correct backend existence fact without claiming service health
repair parent and nested ARM deployment-name collision after failed deploy

promoted_runtime_evidence.claim_boundary: Replacement required; execution and recovery unproven.
Publication resources and browser behavior not deployed.
Partial resources and backend VMs observed; service behavior unverified.
Strategy repaired afterward; fresh isolated What-If required.
Post-failure Azure mutations are not proven. PR #59 repairs code only; fresh planning and separate authority are required.

explicit_unknowns: unpushed local branches and working-tree changes
current Azure CLI tenant and subscription context outside protected artifacts
current actual cost and remaining student credit
current guest health and evidence readability
current effective permissions, policy, and deny assignments
post-PR #59 isolated collector API What-If and post-failure target-resource state
current recovery point and rollback readiness
